description:
The image displays three Very High Resolution (VHR) satellite images of a peri-urban area, demonstrating the change detection process for the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) Urban Atlas (UA) between 2018 and 2021. Each image includes a North arrow and a scale bar indicating 200 m.
The top-left image, labelled "Reference image 2018", shows a mosaic of residential buildings, roads, forested areas, and a large, light-brown, possibly cleared or fallow land plot in the lower central part.
The top-right image, labelled "Reference image 2021", shows the same area with visible changes, including new construction and expansion of built-up areas within the residential zones. The large cleared land plot in the lower center now exhibits distinct linear patterns, suggesting agricultural activity or ground preparation.
The bottom image, labelled "change detection based on VHR (yellow)", is based on the 2021 imagery and highlights areas of detected change with yellow polygons. These polygons delineate new buildings or significant expansions of existing structures, particularly evident in the main residential cluster, near larger buildings on the left side (close to a tennis court), and a small development in the bottom right corner. This visualization exemplifies the use of VHR for identifying urban land use and land cover changes over time.
